providerExtensions.name: Plugin System as Commands Distribution Mechanism
providerExtensions.description: Copilot CLI does not have a standalone "commands" content type equivalent to Claude Code's `/commands` or Cursor's slash commands. Instead, CLI extensibility is delivered via the plugin system: installable packages that bundle agents, skills, hooks, MCP server configs, and (optionally) command directories. Plugins are installed via `copilot plugin install SPECIFICATION` where SPECIFICATION can be a marketplace reference (`plugin@marketplace`), GitHub repo (`OWNER/REPO` or `OWNER/REPO:PATH`), Git URL, or local path. Installed plugins are cached and persist across sessions.

providerExtensions.name: Plugin Manifest (plugin.json)
providerExtensions.description: Every plugin requires a `plugin.json` manifest at the plugin root (also checked at `.plugin/plugin.json`, `.github/plugin/plugin.json`, `.claude-plugin/plugin.json`). The manifest declares the plugin's name (required, kebab-case, max 64 chars), optional metadata (description, version, author, homepage, repository, license, keywords, category, tags), and optional component path overrides (`agents`, `skills`, `commands`, `hooks`, `mcpServers`, `lspServers`). Default conventions are used when path fields are omitted. The `commands` path field in `plugin.json` is explicitly listed in the schema but no documentation exists for the command file format itself.

providerExtensions.name: Plugin Marketplace System
providerExtensions.description: Plugins are discoverable and installable through marketplaces. Two marketplaces are registered by default: `copilot-plugins` (github/copilot-plugins) and `awesome-copilot` (github/awesome-copilot). Custom marketplaces can be added via `copilot plugin marketplace add SPECIFICATION` using the same specification formats as plugin install. A marketplace is defined by a `marketplace.json` file in `.github/plugin/` of a repository or local filesystem. This enables private enterprise plugin distribution without requiring public GitHub repos.
